---
title: "4.11 Declaring and Initializing 2D Arrays"
subtitle: "2D Array Creation and Access"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit4-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Declaring 2D Arrays

### Syntax with two dimensions

2D arrays are declared and created with syntax very similar to 1D arrays, but include 2 dimensions: `[][]`.

The code below creates:

- a 2D array with 2 rows and 3 columns named `ticketInfo`
- a 2D array with 3 rows and 2 columns named `seatingChart`

The number of elements in a 2D array is the number of rows times the number of columns.

## Declaration Syntax

### Source code pattern

```java
// 2D array declaration
// datatype[][] variableName = new datatype[numberRows][numberCols];
int[][] ticketInfo = new int[2][3];
String[][] seatingChart = new String[3][2];
```

## Default Values

### Arrays are objects

Arrays are objects in Java, so any variable that declares an array holds a reference to an object.

If the array has not been created with `new` yet and you try to print the value of the variable, it will print `null`.

When a 2D array is created using `new`, all of its elements are initialized to the default values for the element data type.

## Default Values

### Initial element values

Default values:

| Element type | Default value |
| --- | --- |
| `int` | `0` |
| `double` | `0.0` |
| `boolean` | `false` |
| reference type | `null` |

## Trace Check

### `fillintheblank:: twodaNumElfill`

How many elements are in `ticketInfo`?

```java
int[][] ticketInfo = new int[2][3];
```

Answer:

```text
6
```

Reason: `2 * 3 = 6`.

## Coding Exercise

### `activecode:: twoDArrayCreate`

What will the following code print out?

Can you change `ticketInfo` to be an array of 5 rows and 10 columns?

Can you declare another array called `studentNames` that has 10 rows and 5 columns?

The `length` property of arrays will be explained below.

## TicketInfo Source Code

```java
public class TicketInfo
{
    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        // declare arrays
        int[][] ticketInfo = new int[2][3];
        System.out.println(ticketInfo.length + " rows");
        System.out.println(ticketInfo[0].length + " columns");
    }
}
```

## TicketInfo Test Targets

### Runestone checks

After changing `ticketInfo`, the output should contain:

```text
5 rows
10 columns
```

The code should also contain:

```java
studentNames = new String[10][5]
```

## Initializer Lists for 2D Arrays

### Set values when creating the array

You can also initialize the values for the array when you create it.

In this case you do not need to specify the size of the array; it will be determined from the values you give.

The initializer list used to create and initialize a 2D array consists of 2 initializer lists that represent 1D arrays for each row.

## Initializer List Examples

### Two rows and three rows

The code below creates an array called `ticketInfo` with 2 rows and 3 columns.

It also creates an array called `seatingInfo` with 3 rows and 2 columns.

```java
int[][] ticketInfo = { {25, 20, 25}, {25, 20, 25} };
String[][] seatingInfo = {
    {"Jamal", "Maria"},
    {"Jake", "Suzy"},
    {"Emma", "Luke"}
};
```

## Trace Check

### `fillintheblank:: twodaGetElfill`

What is the value at `seatingInfo[2][1]` after the code executes?

Remember that indices start at `0`, and the first index is for rows and the second index is for columns.

Answer:

```text
Luke
```

The string at row index `2` and column index `1` is `Luke`.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

- declare a 2D array with `datatype[][]`
- create a 2D array with `new datatype[rows][cols]`
- compute total elements as rows times columns
- describe default values after `new`
- read initializer lists as one inner list per row

## End

### 4.11 Declaring and Initializing 2D Arrays

Next: Setting and getting values.
